title: pentaple mantica
intro_study: According to Minerva anestesiologica, pentaple mantica, or mandrake root, benefits include its use in producing anesthesia, as described by Dioscorides, allowing individuals to undergo procedures such as cutting or cauterization without feeling pain.
side_effects: Pentaple mantica can have side effects if used improperly, such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach cramps. In severe cases, overconsumption of the plant's extract can cause respiratory failure, seizures, and even coma. Prolonged exposure to its toxic compounds can also lead to kidney damage and liver failure. Furthermore, improper handling of the plant can cause skin irritation, including rashes, itching, and blistering.
